briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. We will now begin the climate briefing for April 17, 2026.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Nationwide Expansion of Climate Change Week and Carbon Neutrality Practices
sections.content: In today's domestic news, we observed that Climate Change Week, running from April 20 to 24, and Earth Day commemorative events are actively taking place across the nation [1, 9, 10, 12, 13, 14, 18, 20, 22, 23, 30, 31, 37, 39]. Numerous local governments, including Dangjin City, Ulju County, Jinju City, Miryang City, Mokpo City, and Incheon Jung-gu, are encouraging citizen participation through '10-minute lights-out' events and various carbon neutrality practice campaigns [1, 2, 11, 12, 19, 25, 27, 29, 32, 33, 40, 52, 53, 57, 60, 64, 66, 80, 83]. This demonstrates comprehensive efforts to spread a culture of carbon neutrality in daily life, such as Gyeongsangnam-do operating the 18th Climate Change Week under the slogan 'Connecting the World with Carbon Neutrality Practices' [17, 24, 38], indicating widespread efforts to foster a carbon-neutral culture in everyday life [6, 15, 16, 26, 28, 55].

sections.title: 🔴 Escalating Complex Threats of Climate Change
sections.content: Climate change poses complex threats to our society, and warnings have emerged that it could lead to a complex economic crisis by drying up not only water resources but also food and investment [3]. In particular, as heatwaves and cold snaps intensify, increasing housing costs force vulnerable populations to reduce heating and cooling, raising concerns about their health and living conditions [5]. Domestically, the Ulsan region is intensifying surveillance for 'killer tick' infections, responding to climate change-induced health threats [4], and organizations like KORAIL are strengthening on-site safety management to proactively prepare for abnormal summer weather [34, 36]. Internationally, UCLA researchers have highlighted a link between climate change and the rise in homelessness, warning of its expansion into a broader social issue [21, 123], and direct impacts of climate change, such as the disappearance of glaciers in Colombia and the worsening of allergy symptoms, have been reported in various regions, indicating severe effects on human health and ecosystems [113, 115, 122, 128].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Efforts Towards a Carbon-Neutral Society
sections.content: The government is expanding active financial support, including investing 3.4 trillion won, a 14.1% increase from the previous year, in climate change response [8], and various local governments, such as Chungbuk Province and Buan County, are launching 'Climate Crisis Response Committees' and 'Carbon Neutrality and Green Growth Committees' to strengthen their climate crisis response systems [51, 61, 63, 67, 69, 79]. In the industrial sector, the necessity for integrated research that breaks down barriers between sectors has been raised as a key to achieving carbon neutrality [43], and Hyundai Rotem is accelerating its carbon neutrality efforts by developing energy-saving technologies for railway vehicles [78]. In local communities, Gwangmyeong City is awarding up to 7 million won in incentives to excellent carbon-neutral apartment complexes [44, 45, 46], and various public-private cooperation projects are being promoted, such as Hyundai GF Holdings creating a 'Carbon Neutral Forest' [58]. Furthermore, educational programs for future generations, such as Gyeongsan City's 'Visiting Carbon Neutrality Classroom' and Muan County's 'Green Recipe' cooking experience for youth, are actively operating, spreading a culture of carbon neutrality practices [35, 42, 59, 65, 68, 71, 75, 81].

sections.title: 🌍 Opportunities and Challenges of Renewable Energy Transition and International Trends
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy is a crucial task both domestically and internationally, with Siheung City accelerating its shift towards an urban renewable energy city by commencing wind resource analysis following solar [87, 96, 98, 105, 108, 110]. Domestic corporate participation is also expanding, with Kolon Global completing a wind farm in Taebaek [112], and Daewoo E&C expanding its business into the renewable energy sector through an 'engineering alliance' with a Japanese company [88, 101]. However, alongside criticism that renewable energy production in the Busan-Gyeongnam region is stagnant [89], a controversy is emerging in Andong, urging the cancellation of new LNG projects and a shift to renewable energy, illustrating the difficulties in the transition process [90]. Internationally, large-scale investments are continuing, with Amazon securing 1 GW of renewable energy in Australia [56], and the Middle East showing moves to transition from an oil-centric region to a renewable energy hub [106]. Meanwhile, as the importance of renewable energy is highlighted amidst high oil prices, analysis suggests that China could gain an advantage in this sector by setting a goal to double its share of non-fossil energy by 2035 [86, 94, 95, 97], and shifts in energy transition pathways, such as the wavering 'transition fuel' strategy for LNG in the Asian region, are also being observed, signaling changes in the international energy landscape [102, 125, 134].

briefing.closing: In today's briefing, we have examined various domestic and international efforts to address climate change, the reality of the deepening climate crisis, and the opportunities and challenges in the process of transitioning to renewable energy. This is a time when everyone's attention and active participation are becoming even more crucial for a sustainable future. This concludes today's climate briefing.

summary.phenomenon: Colombia has convened a 'voluntary coalition' to address the global fossil fuel deadlock on climate change.
